extraction.one_line_summary: CARAPAZ Richard escaped the early 7-man breakaway with 3.8km to go on the Montevergine summit finish, while PINOT attacked for bonus seconds behind as FROOME struggled.
tactical_patterns: Movistar let Carapaz hunt stage from breakaway despite Betancourt being designated GC leader, revealing internal hierarchy shift.
Bernal attacked final kilometer for bonus seconds while Sky leader Froome was dropped, exposing Sky's inverted team strength.
Carapaz final attack at 3.8km on Montevergine shows breakaway riders must accelerate before final kilometer on medium climbs.

rider_signals.CARAPAZ Richard: positive: First Grand Tour stage win and moving into GC contention, clearly stronger than designated Movistar leader Betancourt
rider_signals.FROOME Chris: negative: Loose handling and nearly crashed on Montevergine climb, required Elissonde and Poels rescue, unconvincing display continues
rider_signals.PINOT Thibaut: positive: Opportunistically attacked for bonus seconds in finale while GC favorites hesitated, gaining time when not expected
rider_signals.BERNAL Egan: positive: Attacked final kilometer for bonus seconds while team leader Froome struggled behind, showing reversed Sky hierarchy
